candidates.title: Americans would rather live next to a nuclear plant than an AI data center, Gallup poll finds
candidates.content: Americans would rather live next to a nuclear plant than an AI data center, Gallup poll finds
candidates.description:
According to a new Gallup poll, 71 percent of Americans oppose building AI data centers near their homes, far more than the 53 percent who object to a nearby nuclear power plant. The top concerns are high water and energy use, pollution, and rising utility costs.
The article Americans would rather l

candidates.title: Ten Chinese firms including ByteDance reportedly get US clearance for AI chips they're not allowed to accept
candidates.content: Ten Chinese firms including ByteDance reportedly get US clearance for AI chips they're not allowed to accept
candidates.description:
The US has cleared roughly ten Chinese companies—including Alibaba, Tencent, and ByteDance—to buy up to 75,000 Nvidia H200 chips each. But not a single chip has shipped. According to Commerce Secretary Lutnick, Beijing is blocking the purchases to protect its domestic chip industry.
The article Ten

candidates.title: Anthropic frames AI competition with China as a now-or-never moment for Washington
candidates.content: Anthropic frames AI competition with China as a now-or-never moment for Washington
candidates.description:
In a policy paper, Anthropic lays out two scenarios for 2028: either the US locks in its compute lead over China, or authoritarian regimes set the rules for the AI era. The timing is no coincidence.
The article Anthropic frames AI competition with China as a now-or-never moment for Washington appear

candidates.title: Arxiv cracks down on unchecked AI-generated content in research papers
candidates.content: Arxiv cracks down on unchecked AI-generated content in research papers
candidates.description:
Arxiv, the influential preprint server where researchers worldwide publish their work before formal peer review, is tightening its rules on AI-generated content.
The article Arxiv cracks down on unchecked AI-generated content in research papers appeared first on The Decoder.

candidates.title: Microsoft pits more than 100 AI agents against each other to find Windows vulnerabilities
candidates.content: Microsoft pits more than 100 AI agents against each other to find Windows vulnerabilities
candidates.description:
Microsoft has built MDASH, a system that pits more than 100 specialized AI agents against each other to find software vulnerabilities. On Patch Tuesday alone, the system uncovered 16 security flaws in Windows, four of them critical. Microsoft isn't saying which AI models power the system.
The articl

candidates.title: OpenAI makes its AI coding assistant Codex available on iOS and Android
candidates.content: OpenAI makes its AI coding assistant Codex available on iOS and Android
candidates.description:
OpenAI brings its AI coding assistant Codex to the ChatGPT app on iOS and Android. 
The article OpenAI makes its AI coding assistant Codex available on iOS and Android appeared first on The Decoder.

candidates.title: x.AI plays catch-up with Grok Build, its first terminal-based coding agent
candidates.content: x.AI plays catch-up with Grok Build, its first terminal-based coding agent
candidates.description:
Elon Musk's AI company x.AI is jumping into the coding agent space with Grok Build, a new terminal-based tool.
The article x.AI plays catch-up with Grok Build, its first terminal-based coding agent appeared first on The Decoder.

candidates.title: Microsoft pulls Claude Code licenses and pushes developers back toward its own AI tool
candidates.content: Microsoft pulls Claude Code licenses and pushes developers back toward its own AI tool
candidates.description:
Thousands of Microsoft developers used Anthropic's Claude Code for programming. Now the company is revoking licenses and betting on GitHub Copilot CLI.
The article Microsoft pulls Claude Code licenses and pushes developers back toward its own AI tool appeared first on The Decoder.
